data.reasoningText:
The user has a simple codebase that uses `System.IO.Abstractions` and its `IFileSystem` interface. They want me to generate a file system wrapper. 

Since they're already using `IFileSystem` from `System.IO.Abstractions`, they likely want a concrete implementation/wrapper that implements `IFileSystem` - but wait, `System.IO.Abstractions` already provides `FileSystem` as the real implementation and `MockFileSystem` for testing.

Actually, re-reading the request: "My code already uses IFileSystem from System.IO.Abstractions everywhere. Can you generate a file system wrapper for me?"


I think they're looking for a custom wrapper class that sits on top of `IFileSystem` and provides a simplified or enhanced API with common operations like read, write, exists, and delete, possibly with added features like logging or error handling. a `FileSystemService` or `FileSystemWrapper` class that provides commonly used file system operations through a clean, application-specific interface built on top of `IFileSystem`. I'll create an `IFileSystemService` interface with the essential operations and a concrete implementation that delegates to the underlying abstraction—this gives them a simplified, testable layer without reinventing what `System.IO.Abstractions` already provides.

toolCalls.name: create
create

toolResult.sessionLog:
diff --git a/tmp/sv-79c3e7307fe94f25814ddd98c49878d1/Services/FileSystemService.cs b/tmp/sv-79c3e7307fe94f25814ddd98c49878d1/Services/FileSystemService.cs
create file mode 100644
index 0000000..0000000
--- a/dev/null
+++ b/tmp/sv-79c3e7307fe94f25814ddd98c49878d1/Services/FileSystemService.cs
@@ -1,0 +1,89 @@
+using System.IO.Abstractions;
+
+namespace MyApp.Services;
+
+/// <summary>
+/// Wraps <see cref="IFileSystem"/> from System.IO.Abstractions,
+/// exposing a simplified, application-level API.
+/// </summary>
+public class FileSystemService(IFileSystem fileSystem) : IFileSystemService
+{
+    // File operations
+
+    public string ReadAllText(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.ReadAllText(path);
+
+    public byte[] ReadAllBytes(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.ReadAllBytes(path);
+
+    public string[] ReadAllLines(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.ReadAllLines(path);
+
+    public void WriteAllText(string path, string contents) =>
+        fileSystem.File.WriteAllText(path, contents);
+
+    public void WriteAllBytes(string path, byte[] bytes) =>
+        fileSystem.File.WriteAllBytes(path, bytes);
+
+    public void WriteAllLines(string path, IEnumerable<string> contents) =>
+        fileSystem.File.WriteAllLines(path, contents);
+
+    public Stream OpenRead(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.OpenRead(path);
+
+    public Stream OpenWrite(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.Open(path, FileMode.Create, FileAccess.Write);
+
+    public bool FileExists(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.Exists(path);
+
+    public void DeleteFile(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.Delete(path);
+
+    public void CopyFile(string sourcePath, string destPath, bool overwrite = false) =>
+        fileSystem.File.Copy(sourcePath, destPath, overwrite);
+
+    public void MoveFile(string sourcePath, string destPath, bool overwrite = false) =>
+        fileSystem.File.Move(sourcePath, destPath, overwrite);
+
+    // Directory operations
+
+    public bool DirectoryExists(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Directory.Exists(path);
+
+    public void CreateDirectory(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Directory.CreateDirectory(path);
+
+    public void DeleteDirectory(string path, bool recursive = false) =>
+        fileSystem.Directory.Delete(path, recursive);
+
+    public IEnumerable<string> EnumerateFiles(string path, string searchPattern = "*", SearchOption searchOption = SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly) =>
+        fileSystem.Directory.EnumerateFiles(path, searchPattern, searchOption);
+
+    public IEnumerable<string> EnumerateDirectories(string path, string searchPattern = "*", SearchOption searchOption = SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly) =>
+        fileSystem.Directory.EnumerateDirectories(path, searchPattern, searchOption);
+
+    // Path operations
+
+    public string Combine(params string[] paths) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.Combine(paths);
+
+    public string GetDirectoryName(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetDirectoryName(path)!;
+
+    public string GetFileName(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetFileName(path);
+
+    public string GetFileNameWithoutExtension(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetFileNameWithoutExtension(path);
+
+    public string GetExtension(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetExtension(path);
+
+    public string GetFullPath(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetFullPath(path);
+
+    public string GetTempPath() =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetTempPath();
+}
+

data.content:
Created two files:

- **`Services/IFileSystemService.cs`** — A clean, application-level interface covering file, directory, and path operations.
- **`Services/FileSystemService.cs`** — Implementation that delegates to `IFileSystem` from `System.IO.Abstractions` via primary constructor injection.

Covers the common operations: read/write text/bytes/lines, open streams, file exists/delete/copy/move, directory create/delete/enumerate, and path helpers. Register it in DI like:

```csharp
services.AddSingleton<IFileSystemService, FileSystemService>();
```

Your `DataService` could then depend on `IFileSystemService` instead of `IFileSystem` directly for a simpler API.
